_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Visual-regression test layer for rendered HTML and PDF documents

steps.description: Backend: PDF visual-regression module. Add Pillow + pixelmatch to [dependency-groups] dev in pyproject.toml; regenerate uv.lock. Create tests/visual/__init__.py + tests/visual/test_pdf_visual_regression.py that enumerates baseline PDFs under tests/visual/baselines/pdfs/<doc>/source.pdf, rasterises each via the pdftoppm system binary, and pixel-diffs each rendered page against the committed baseline PNG at tests/visual/baselines/pdfs/<doc>/page-NNN.png using Pillow + pixelmatch. Pick the pixel-tolerance value from the InnoForge precedent (iw-doc-plan/main/iw-doc-plan/) — search for visual-regression / pdftoppm / pixelmatch; if InnoForge has no precedent, default to maxDiffPixels = 0.5%% of total page pixels with a 0.1 per-pixel threshold. Record the chosen value + rationale in the S01 report notes. Populate 4–8 baseline PDFs spanning the editorial categories enumerated from doc-system/ (architecture, infrastructure, blog, pitch-deck, promo, research, release-notes, user-guide). Pre-rasterise the baseline PNGs and commit them alongside the source PDFs. Failure path: write tests/output/visual-diff/<doc>-page<N>-{actual,baseline,diff}.png and include the absolute *-diff.png path in the pytest.fail() message. Add `make visual-regression-pdf` target to Makefile. RED-first evidence: deliberately introduce a 1-pixel shift in one baseline PNG, re-run the target, capture the failing output + *-diff.png path; then revert and confirm green. Record both runs as tdd_red_evidence. Pre-flight gates (make format / typecheck / lint) must pass. Targeted test verification only: `uv run pytest tests/visual/test_pdf_visual_regression.py -v` — do NOT run make test-unit or make test-integration (S10/S11 own those). Do NOT touch production code under orch/, dashboard/, executor/. Do NOT add any alembic migration.

Backend: HTML visual-regression module + umbrella make target. Extend tests/e2e/playwright_wrapper.py with a screenshot_to_baseline(url, output_path, *, session=None) helper that wraps `playwright-cli kill-all` → `playwright-cli open <url>` → `playwright-cli screenshot` (saves to .playwright-cli/page-<ts>.png — no path arg accepted) → move file to output_path. Use existing subprocess-wrapper patterns; NEVER call chromium.launch() directly, NEVER use agent-browser, NEVER npx playwright install, NEVER edit .playwright/cli.config.json. Create tests/visual/test_html_visual_regression.py that enumerates baseline HTML docs under tests/visual/baselines/html/<category>/source.html, drives the dashboard test server (match existing dashboard E2E patterns), captures via screenshot_to_baseline(), then diffs against tests/visual/baselines/html/<category>/baseline.png using the SAME Pillow + pixelmatch diff helper S01 introduced (DRY — import or share; do NOT copy-paste). Populate the remaining baseline slots so total PDF+HTML baseline count lands between 8 and 15. Pre-capture each baseline PNG and commit. Failure path same as S01 (write *-actual / *-baseline / *-diff PNGs; include diff path in pytest.fail()). Skip with clear message if playwright-cli missing. Add `make visual-regression-html` target and `make visual-regression` umbrella target chaining both halves. RED-first evidence: deliberately shift one HTML baseline PNG, re-run, capture failing output + *-diff.png path; revert; confirm green; also run `make visual-regression` umbrella once at end to confirm both halves wire together. Pre-flight gates must pass. Targeted test verification only: `uv run pytest tests/visual/test_html_visual_regression.py -v` and one umbrella `make visual-regression`. Do NOT run make test-unit or make test-integration. Do NOT touch production code under orch/, dashboard/, executor/. Do NOT add any alembic migration. Depends on S01 for the shared diff helper and the shared pixel-tolerance constant.

Backend: CI workflow + docs + skill + tracker. Create .github/workflows/visual-regression.yml with three triggers — cron (nightly, e.g. `0 3 * * *` or matching existing nightly cadence), workflow_dispatch (manual), and pull_request with `paths` filter on dashboard/static/styles.css, dashboard/templates/pdf/**, dashboard/templates/components/doc_*, doc-system/**. Job steps: checkout, install uv (match existing workflow pattern), `uv sync --all-groups`, ensure pdftoppm/poppler-utils available, install playwright-cli per project pattern, run `make visual-regression`, on failure upload tests/output/visual-diff/** as job artefact for reviewer download. Ship with `continue-on-error: true` for a 1-week burn-in window and an inline `# BURN-IN: flip to continue-on-error: false after 2026-06-01` comment. Update docs/IW_AI_Core_Testing_Strategy.md: §2 add `Layer 8 — visual regression` subsection (model on existing Layer N), §5 add new gate row for `make visual-regression` (CI-only, nightly + path-filtered, not in `make check`), §9 flip the visual-regression row from ❌ to ✅ with CR-00082 + 2026-05-24. Update skills/iw-ai-core-testing/SKILL.md: add a new section `Visual regression — patterns and baseline-management rules` covering when to add a baseline, how to update one (review-gated PR), forbidden auto-accept pattern, Playwright CLI rules (link back to CLAUDE.md), shared-constant pixel-tolerance discipline. Then run `uv run iw sync-skills --force iw-ai-core-testing` and record the sync output in the report notes (confirms .claude/skills/iw-ai-core-testing/ is byte-equal to the master copy). Update ai-dev/work/TESTS_ENHANCEMENT.md: §8 row 4.1 flip TODO → DONE with notes column `CR-00082 / 2026-05-24 / 4-PDF + 4-HTML baselines, nightly + path-filtered CI, blocking after 1-week burn-in`; add a new v1.4 header entry dated 2026-05-24 summarising the visual-regression delivery and pointing at CR-00082. Pre-flight gates: format / lint (typecheck N/A — yaml + markdown only; record as skipped:no-code-changes). Targeted verification: `make visual-regression` (must still pass). Do NOT run make test-unit or make test-integration. Do NOT touch production code under orch/, dashboard/, executor/. Do NOT add any alembic migration.

Per-agent review covering S01 + S02 + S03 in a single pass. Pre-review gate: `make lint` + `make format-check` show zero new violations in this CR's changed files. Scope: diff against main MUST touch only paths inside scope.allowed_paths (tests/visual/**, tests/e2e/playwright_wrapper.py, Makefile, pyproject.toml, uv.lock, .github/workflows/visual-regression.yml, docs/IW_AI_Core_Testing_Strategy.md, skills/iw-ai-core-testing/**, .claude/skills/iw-ai-core-testing/**, ai-dev/work/TESTS_ENHANCEMENT.md, ai-dev/active/CR-00082/**). Any file under orch/, dashboard/, executor/, scripts/, bin/, or orch/db/migrations/versions/** is a CRITICAL scope-creep finding. Playwright CLI compliance (AC6): grep tests/visual/ and the extended playwright_wrapper.py for chromium.launch / agent-browser / npx playwright install — any match is CRITICAL. Pixel-tolerance discipline: S01 picks ONE tolerance constant; S02 reuses it via import/shared helper. A second hardcoded value is HIGH. Baseline-management discipline (AC4 + Design Notes): total baseline count 8–15; every editorial category covered; the test code contains NO branch that writes to tests/visual/baselines/** (no auto-accept) — any such branch is CRITICAL. CI workflow correctness (AC5): three triggers (cron + workflow_dispatch + pull_request with the exact paths filter); on-failure artefact upload of tests/output/visual-diff/**; burn-in comment naming 2026-06-01. Docs/skill/tracker (AC7): §2 Layer 8, §5 gate row, §9 ✅ with CR-00082 + 2026-05-24; skill master and .claude/skills/iw-ai-core-testing/SKILL.md byte-equal (verify via diff); §8 row 4.1 = DONE, v1.4 header entry. Failure path (AC3): pytest.fail() messages include absolute *-diff.png paths; both S01 and S02 tdd_red_evidence describe the deliberate-regression run AND the revert. RED evidence (TDD §5a): S01 + S02 record the deliberate-regression pattern; S03 records `n/a — CI yaml + docs + skill + tracker edits only`. Dependency / lockfile: pyproject.toml adds Pillow + pixelmatch under [dependency-groups] dev; uv.lock regenerated and consistent. Test verification: `make visual-regression` and `uv run pytest tests/visual/ -v` both pass. Do NOT run the full unit/integration suites.

Global cross-agent final review covering AC1–AC8. AC1: `make visual-regression-pdf` passes against committed baselines. AC2: `make visual-regression-html` passes against committed baselines. AC3: forced-regression produces *-actual/*-baseline/*-diff PNGs under tests/output/visual-diff/ and the failure message names the *-diff.png path. AC4: baseline count 8–15 with every editorial category from doc-system/ represented. AC5: .github/workflows/visual-regression.yml has cron + workflow_dispatch + pull_request paths-filter triggers. AC6: zero chromium.launch / agent-browser / npx playwright install / .playwright/cli.config.json edits in the diff (grep the full diff). AC7: docs §2 Layer 8 + §5 gate row + §9 ✅; skill section present and synced; tracker §8 row 4.1 DONE + v1.4 header. AC8: scope-clean diff (git diff --name-only main...HEAD matches scope.allowed_paths). Cross-step consistency: S01's tolerance constant is shared (not duplicated); S01's diff helper is reused by S02 (no copy-paste); S03's CI workflow invokes `make visual-regression` and that target exists. Doc/skill/tracker internal consistency: §9 count matches reality, §2 layer count is 8, skill master byte-equal to .claude/skills/ copy (`diff` returns empty), tracker §8 + v1.4 + CR-ID all match. Architecture: tests/visual/ is a NEW sibling of tests/unit/ etc. — verify it is NOT collected by `make test-unit` or `make test-integration` (check pyproject.toml `[tool.pytest.ini_options].testpaths`); accidental inclusion is CRITICAL (would inflate daemon gate wall-clock). Security: no hardcoded credentials in CI yaml; no pull_request_target trigger. Test verification: full `make test-unit` + `make test-integration` + `make visual-regression` — all three must pass; regression in unit/integration is CRITICAL (new tests should be invisible to them).
QV: Assertion scanner (forbid new vacuous tests)
QV: Unit tests (new tests/visual/ module should be invisible to this gate)
QV: Integration tests (new tests/visual/ module should be invisible to this gate)
QV: Diff coverage (new/changed lines must be well-covered)
QV: Secret scanning (gitleaks) — no secrets expected; tests/CI/docs-only change

Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill. Look for: (a) pixel-tolerance churn (did S01 pick a value that S02 had to override — sign InnoForge precedent doesn't map cleanly); (b) baseline-asset friction (git LFS surprises, large-file warnings); (c) Playwright CLI rule violations causing retries (suggest stronger upfront callout in prompts); (d) pdftoppm/poppler-utils availability in CI (image gap); (e) which QV gates burned fix cycles and why (especially diff-coverage on new test files); (f) cross-agent integration friction between S01/S02 (make-target naming) and S03 (CI invocation).
